citation_or_search_trail: CMS-PAS-LUM-20-001 public summary, 2017 full-year luminosity 42.12 +/- 0.34 fb^-1; used as a scale reference for the user-provided 10 fb^-1 subset.
sources.evaluation_method: Log-normal normalization nuisance using public CMS 2017 luminosity uncertainty as scale reference for the user-provided 10 fb^-1 subset.
sources.ref_2: CMS-PAS-LUM-20-001 public summary, 2017 full-year luminosity 42.12 +/- 0.34 fb^-1; used as a scale reference for the user-provided 10 fb^-1 subset.

affected_templates_processes: all signal and background MC templates
citation_or_search_trail: Phase 3 cut-motivation closure: largest final-state lepton-ID data/MC step-efficiency discrepancy is about 3 percent; propagated as a rate envelope.
sources.evaluation_method: Closure-derived rate envelope from Phase 3 trigger/flavor-ID data/MC step efficiencies.
sources.ref_2: Phase 3 cut-motivation closure: largest final-state lepton-ID data/MC step-efficiency discrepancy is about 3 percent; propagated as a rate envelope.

citation_or_search_trail: CMS-HIG-16-041 and CMS-HIG-19-001 normalize H(125) signal to SM expectations; prompt effective signal cross sections are user-provided, so a 5 percent composition prior is scanned and marked fallback.
sources.evaluation_method: Fallback signal production composition normsys, propagated separately for ggH, VBF, and VH groups.
sources.ref_2: CMS-HIG-16-041 and CMS-HIG-19-001 normalize H(125) signal to SM expectations; prompt effective signal cross sections are user-provided, so a 5 percent composition prior is scanned and marked fallback.

citation_or_search_trail: Open-data fallback for prompt effective ZZ cross section; CMS references estimate ZZ from simulation and treat background normalization as a systematic source.
sources.evaluation_method: Fallback qqZZ normalization normsys on the prompt-effective qqZZ template.
sources.ref_2: Open-data fallback for prompt effective ZZ cross section; CMS references estimate ZZ from simulation and treat background normalization as a systematic source.

citation_or_search_trail: Open-data fallback for small loop-induced ggZZ component; prior is wider than qqZZ because only prompt effective cross sections are available in this sandbox.
sources.evaluation_method: Fallback ggZZ normalization normsys on the prompt-effective loop-induced ZZ template.
sources.ref_2: Open-data fallback for small loop-induced ggZZ component; prior is wider than qqZZ because only prompt effective cross sections are available in this sandbox.

citation_or_search_trail: Phase 3 sideband fake diagnostics show only 11 DY+jets raw entries in the two sidebands after selection, so the DY fake proxy is weakly constrained and assigned a broad fallback prior.
sources.evaluation_method: Broad DY fake-proxy normsys from low sideband statistics and DY-only fake-model limitation.
sources.ref_2: Phase 3 sideband fake diagnostics show only 11 DY+jets raw entries in the two sidebands after selection, so the DY fake proxy is weakly constrained and assigned a broad fallback prior.

citation_or_search_trail: Phase 3 TTBar/DY weighted-yield diagnostic in the signal window; TTBar is below promotion threshold and propagated as an omission envelope on reducible background.
sources.evaluation_method: Omission-envelope normsys from Phase 3 TTBar/DY signal-window ratio.
sources.ref_2: Phase 3 TTBar/DY weighted-yield diagnostic in the signal window; TTBar is below promotion threshold and propagated as an omission envelope on reducible background.

citation_or_search_trail: CMS momentum-scale public performance context reports per-mille-level deviations; propagated by shifting m4l templates by +/-0.1 percent.
sources.evaluation_method: Histosys shape variation from refilling templates with m4l scaled by +/-0.1 percent.
sources.ref_2: CMS momentum-scale public performance context reports per-mille-level deviations; propagated by shifting m4l templates by +/-0.1 percent.

citation_or_search_trail: Derived from Phase 3 per-bin sumw2 templates. Implemented as group/category normalization nuisances and tested with alternative-bin stability because full per-bin staterror profiling is computationally impractical in this sandbox.
sources.evaluation_method: Grouped group/category normsys from Phase 3 sumw2; not a full bin-by-bin staterror model.
sources.ref_2: Derived from Phase 3 per-bin sumw2 templates. Implemented as group/category normalization nuisances and tested with alternative-bin stability because full per-bin staterror profiling is computationally impractical in this sandbox.

citation_or_search_trail: phase2_strategy/outputs/STRATEGY.md [A2]/[SP2] requires user-provided fallback handling when public/campaign matches are unavailable.
sources.evaluation_method: Phase 3 recorded the prompt xsec, metadata denominator, and nominal weight for every MC sample; Phase 4a propagates per-process normalization nuisances rather than treating the prompt xsecs as independently verified public cross sections.
sources.nominal_or_variation_size: Per-sample user-prompt effective cross sections and metadata denominators; see prompt_xsec_records.
sources.ref_2: Prompt/user-provided effective cross sections retained with per-process normalization nuisances.
sources.this_analysis: Implemented through the nominal MC weights plus signal/background normalization nuisance rows.

affected_templates_processes: classifier inputs only; no nominal fit templates because nPV/PV variables are not used in the Phase 4a fit
sources.evaluation_method: Phase 3 input validation excluded pvNdof under [A6]; no classifier categories are promoted and no PV-dependent reweighting is applied, so no Phase 4a template nuisance is propagated.
sources.ref_2: pvNdof is explicitly excluded by Phase 2 [A6]; no PV variable enters the nominal fit.
sources.this_analysis: Documented and not propagated because PV variables are not used in nominal categories or templates.

sources.evaluation_method: No fiducial/inclusive cross-section conversion is performed in Phase 4a.
sources.ref_2: PDG H->ZZ* fraction retained for cross-section conversions
sources.this_analysis: Not used because Phase 4a reports detector-level mu only

sources.evaluation_method: S2 classifier categories failed promotion gates and are not used in the nominal fit.
Angular closure is retained from Phase 3, but angular inputs are not used after S2 rejection.
sources.ref_2: Angular inputs not used in nominal fit after S2 rejection
